MASTER MOTION CALENDAR ON MAR-17-2022 IN 206, PLAINTIFF'S UNOPPOSED MOTION TO CONTINUE TRIAL AND DISCOVERY AND ALL TRIAL RELATED DEADLINES. DENISE TOP, ESQ. APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF AND SPECIALLY APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT VIA COURTCALL. THE COURT GRANTS PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO CONTINUE TRIAL. THE TRIAL DATE OF 5/16/22 IS VACATED AND THE NEW TRIAL DATE IS 9/12/22 AT 9:30AM IN DEPT. 206. ALL PRETRIAL AND DISCOVERY DEADLINES SHALL TRACK THE NEW TRIAL DATE. THE COURT DID NOT RECEIVE COURTESY COPIES OF THE MOTION WHICH IS IN VIOLATION OF SF LOCAL RULE 2.7B. THE COURT ORDERS PLAINTIFF TO PAY $60.00 FILING FEE VIA E-FILING NO LATER THAN 4:00PM TODAY WHICH IS THE COST FOR FILING PAPERS THAT PLAINTIFF WOULD HAVE TO FILE AGAIN WITH A COURTESY COPY IN ORDER TO GET THE 9/12/22 TRIAL DATE. NOTICE OF TRIAL IS WAIVED. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER THAT CONFORMS TO THE COURT'S RULING AND SHOULD BE APPROVED AS TO FORM. JUDGE: SAMUEL K. FENG; CLERK: R. VELUZ; COURT REPORTER: ANDY RODRIGUEZ, CSR #14226, TEL. (562)419-4594, EMAIL: ANDYRODRIGUEZCSR@GMAIL.COM. (206)

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR# 8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com mailto:maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. (302/RBU)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order (Part 2 Of 2 For Purposes Of Entry Of Tentative Ruling)
Off Calendar Mar-16-2022 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order
Off Calendar Mar-16-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: Defendant The Ignatian Corporation dba St. Ignatius College Preparatory's Motion For Protective Order.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The Motion is DENIED and GRANTED in part, as follows. A protective order prohibiting the deposition of a corporate president may be granted where it is shown he or she lacks knowledge or involvement in the litigation, and such deposition is being sought prior to plaintiff's exhaustion of less intrusive means of discovery. Such "high level" (or "apex") depositions "raise a tremendous potential for discovery abuse and harassment." Liberty Mut. Ins. Co. v. Sup.Ct. (Frysinger) (1992) 10 CA4th 1282, 1287-1288 (analogizing to federal cases and citing text). Here, Plaintiff has presented evidence that Father Reese has knowledge and involvement in the decision to not offer Plaintiff a contract for 2019-2020 as follows: (1) Michelle Levine needed Father Reese's approval to offer Plaintiff a contract for 2019-2020 (Mtn., Decl., Ex. G, 108:1-11), and (2) Father Reese attended an in-person meeting where a discussion took place about whether Plaintiff would be offered a contract for 2019-2020 (Mtn., Decl., Ex. G, 95:19-22). Plaintiff has undertaken less intrusive means including the depositions of Evans, Bettencourt, and Levine. Separately, Defendant raised the issue of the ministerial exception in the November 4, 2021 MSJ. The deposition of Father Reese is limited in scope to (1) the in-person meeting described above; (2) the decision to not offer Plaintiff a contract for 2019-2020; and (3) "the religious relationships he manages as President and how they affect SI as a Jesuit, non-Archdiocese school" (Opp., 17:2-3). Plaintiff has not presented any evidence that Father Reese has relevant personal knowledge beyond these topics. All requests for sanctions are denied.

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem at Peter.Catalanotti@wilsonelser.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Peter Catalanotti; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)
